data.title: Woolworths Target Price Raised 0.2% to A$31.90/Share by Bell Potter>WOW.AU
Private Land and Property Fund's Manager Confirms Buy Back of Up to 70,000 Units
data.description:
Private Land and Property Fund manager, Booster Investment Management, said it will buy back up to 70,000 of the company's units via the New Zealand's Exchange on July 1 at a price not exceeding the latest net asset value, according to a Monday filing with the New Zealand bourse.

Shares of the company rose nearly 2% in recent Monday trade.

data.title: Fortior Technology Launches Up to HK$1.96 Billion Hong Kong IPO
data.description:
Fortior Technology (Shenzhen) (HKG:1304) looks to raise up to HK$1.96 billion from its Hong Kong initial public offering, as the city remains on track to become the number one IPO destination in 2025.

The integrated circuit design company is offering 16,299,500 shares at a maximum offer price of HK$120.50, according to an early Monday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The shares are expected to be priced on July 7, with the allocation results to be disclosed the next day, in time for the company's trading debut on the bourse on July 9.

Fortior Technology attracted $112.0 million in cornerstone investments from 10 investors, who agreed to subscribe to 44.76% of the shares on offer.

Net proceeds from the offering, amounting to approximately HK$1.85 billion, will be used for the enhancement of the company's R&D and innovation capabilities, for further enrichment of its product portfolio, for the expansion of its sales network overseas, for strategic investments, and for working capital and general corporate purposes.

China International Capital Corporation Hong Kong Securities acts as sole sponsor, sponsor-overall coordinator, overall coordinator, joint global coordinator, joint bookrunner and joint lead manager of the manager of the deal, while BOCI Asia and GF Securities (Hong Kong) Brokerage are overall coordinators, joint global coordinators, joint bookrunners and joint lead managers.

data.title: WA1 Resources Reports Indicated Mineral Resource for Western Australia Niobium Deposit
data.description:
WA1 Resources reported an indicated mineral resource estimate of 73 million tonnes at 1.38% niobium pentoxide, including a high-grade subset of 31 million tonnes at 2.31% niobium pentoxide for the Luni niobium deposit at its West Arunta project in Western Australia, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The deposit's total mineral resource estimate, indicated and inferred, has increased by 10% to 220 million tonnes at 1% niobium pentoxide, including a high-grade subset of 53 million tonnes at 2.2% niobium pentoxide

The mineral resource estimate is constrained to shallow, weathered mineralisation, starting from 30 meters below surface and extending to a maximum depth of 180 meters, with mineralisation open at depth.

Resource-focused drilling activities during the year are aiming to define better key zones and further increase resource confidence in select high-grade areas, the filing added.

data.title: Star Entertainment gets notice to end project stake selloff deal with HK shareholders
data.description:
Australia's Star Entertainment said on Monday it had received a notice from Hong Kong's Far East Consortium International and Chow Tai Fook Enterprises to terminate the agreement for selling its 50% stake in its Queen's Wharf project in Brisbane.

The agreement was entered into by the parties with the embattled casino operator on March 7, with the termination set to take effect five business days from June 30, unless withdrawn earlier.

data.title: China's Zijin Mining forks out $1.2 billion for gold mine in Kazakhstan
data.description: China's Zijin Mining said on Monday it has agreed to buy one of the largest gold mines of Kazakhstan, the Raygorodok Gold Mine, for $1.2 billion.

data.title: Black Canyon Confirms Manganese, Iron Mineralization at Western Australia Project
data.description:
Black Canyon confirmed the presence of large manganese and iron mineralization at the Wandanya project in Western Australia, according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

Multiple reverse circulation drill holes intersected the hydrothermal stratabound manganese horizon, confirming the consistency of mineralization and geology previously encountered, the filing said.

Further drilling is planned to extend and define targets in August, according to the filing.

data.title: KKR to Sell Stake in Indian Pharma Company for $1.4B
data.description:
By P.R. Venkat

Private equity firm KKR has agreed to sell its entire stake in India-listed J.B. Chemicals & Pharmaceuticals for $1.4 billion.

Under the deal, KKR will sell its 46.39% stake to Torrent Pharmaceuticals, the private-equity firm said late Sunday. The two pharmaceutical firms will then merge through a scheme of arrangement.

After J.B.'s merger with Torrent, each shareholder owning 100 shares of J.B. will receive 51 shares of Torrent.

"This strategic alignment furthers our goal of strengthening our presence in the Indian pharma market, and build a larger diversified global presence," Samir Mehta, Executive Chairman, Torrent said.

J.B., whose majority of revenue comes from the Indian market, also has a presence in Russia and South Africa. The company exports its finished formulations to over 40 countries, including the USA. It has eight manufacturing facilities in India, including a dedicated lozenge manufacturing facility.

Torrent Pharma concentrates on the chronic and sub-chronic therapeutic segments. It also has a global presence in countries like Brazil, Germany, and the U.S.

Write to P.R. Venkat at venkat.pr@wsj.com

data.title: West African Resources  Says First Gold Poured at Burkina Faso Project
data.description:
West African Resources said first gold was poured ahead of schedule at the Kiaka gold project in Burkina Faso, with the maiden gold bars weighing 5.7 kilograms, according to a Monday ASX filing.

Around 140,000 tonnes at 0.7 grams per tonne grade of gold were processed through the Kiaka processing plant, with indicative metallurgical recoveries over 92%. It expects to ramp it up to nameplate throughput during the third quarter.

It also ramped up open-pit mining at Kiaka Main and Kiaka South pits.

data.title: Nikkei May Rise, Tracking Wall Street's Gains — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks may rise, tracking Wall Street's gains on Friday. Domestic technology shares could advance after the Nasdaq Composite hit a record high. Sentiment may also be buoyed by Commerce Secretary Lutnick and Treasury Secretary Bessent who said that the U.S. was closing in numerous trade deals. Nikkei futures are 335 points higher at 40575 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 144.48, compared with 144.66 in late New York trading on Friday. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 1.4% to close at 40150.79 on Friday, its highest closing level since Dec. 27. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: New Zealand Business Count Falls in May
data.description:
The total business count across New Zealand fell to 591,693 in May from 596,721 in April, according to data from Stats NZ on Monday.

The enterprise entries showed a decline to 5,595 in May from 8,619 in the previous month.

Meanwhile, the number of enterprise exits rose to 10,620 in May from 5,835 in April.

data.title: Brickworks Announces More U.S. Impairments Amid Soft Demand — Update
data.description:
By Stuart Condie

SYDNEY--Brickworks will impair the value of its U.S. operations for the third time in less than a year after regional conditions stayed softer than the Australian brickmaker had anticipated.

The ASX-listed company on Monday said it would impair the value of its North America building-products business by 102 million Australian dollars, equivalent to US$66.6 million. It estimated the post-tax impairment at A$75 million.

It already announced more than A$100 million of post-tax impairments against the value of the North America business since July 2024, citing lower-than-expected demand and policy driven economic turmoil.

"Market conditions have remained significantly more subdued than anticipated," Brickworks said Monday. "Factors impacting the market have included continued elevated interest rates, high construction costs and suppressed consumer confidence."

Brickworks, which entered the U.S. in 2018 with the US$110 million acquisition of brick maker Glen-Gery, said it expects marginally positive North America earnings for the 12 months through July.

It recorded a A$3 million earnings loss against the unit over its fiscal first half, which ended Jan. 31.

The company expects to report second-half earnings at its domestic building-products business slightly ahead of the A$50 million seen in the first half.

Second-half property earnings will exceed the A$38 million reported for the six months through January, helped by a A$65 million revaluation gain on the industrial property trust it co-owns with Goodman. It will also record a A$39 million development profit.

Write to Stuart Condie at stuart.condie@wsj.com

data.title: Nine to Acquire EPL Soccer Rights as SingTel's Optus Refocuses
data.description:
By Stuart Condie

SYDNEY--Media conglomerate Nine Entertainment agreed to acquire Australian streaming rights to English Premier League soccer from Optus, allowing the Singapore Telecommunications-owned telecom provider to refocus on core services.

Nine on Monday said it would pay an upfront 20 million Australian dollars, equivalent to US$13.1 million, for the right to stream the EPL and FA Cup competitions.

It will contribute further through the rights cycle, while also receiving undisclosed payments from Optus through 2028.

Nine expects the acquisition to be positive on an earnings and cash basis to its Stan streaming platform over the term of the rights, which expire in mid-2028.

Write to Stuart Condie at stuart.condie@wsj.com

data.title: Singapore's renewables usage hits record high as imports, solar output rise
data.description:
Singapore boosted the share of renewables in its power generation mix to a record high in May, an analysis of the latest market data showed, as the country ramped up renewable imports and accelerated local solar power generation.

Domestic solar generation in May rose at the fastest pace since March 2024 and renewable imports rose a third straight month to their highest in more than two years, lifting the share of renewables in the city-state's power mix to 2.58%, data from the National Electricity Market of Singapore showed.

Cross-border power trade is seen as key to easing regional reliance on fossil fuels amid growing data centre-driven power demand.

Singapore expects to meet 6 GW, or around one-third of its power demand from clean electricity imports by 2035, as Asia's second-smallest country has limited renewable energy potential. Gas-fired power plants in Singapore account for about 95% of its power capacity.

In the five months through May, the data showed Singapore imported 122.7 million kilowatt-hours of clean power, or 0.52% of total generation, the data showed.

It did not import any power during the same period last year, the data showed, and only started importing small quantities in the last quarter of 2024.

The share of imports in Singapore's power mix rose for a third straight month in May, displacing some fossil fuel-fired generation. Singapore's total electricity generation grew 0.4% during the first five months, the data showed.

Singapore has two active cross-border power trade deals: the 200 MW Lao PDR–Thailand–Malaysia–Singapore (LTMS) and the 50 MW Energy Exchange Malaysia (ENEGEM) pilot project with Malaysia's state utility Tenaga Nasional Berhad.

The Singapore Energy Market Authority's chief executive said in October the terms of an extension to the LTMS had yet to be finalised, as Singapore was waiting for Thailand to finalise details on transmission charges for the multilateral deal.

On Friday, the EMA told Reuters in a statement that discussions were "ongoing for future enhancements to the LTMS," without elaborating further.

data.title: IFBH Rakes in HK$1.1 Billion Net Proceeds from Oversubscribed Hong Kong IPO; Prices Offering at Top End of Range
data.description:
IFBH (HKG:6603) raised HK$1.07 billion in net proceeds from its initial public offering in Hong Kong as the Singapore-incorporated company priced the shares at the top end of the price band.

The beverage maker priced the shares at HK$27.80 apiece, the upper limit of the guidance of as low as HK$25.30 per share, according to a Friday after-market hours filing.

The Hong Kong public offering was 2,682.35 times subscribed, triggering a clawback of 16,666,600 shares from the international offer, which took the total number of shares offered under this portion to 20,833,400 from 4,166,800 initially.

The international offering was 22.93 times and the company offered 20,833,400 shares, down 37,500,000 shares initially following the reallocation.

IFBH noted that it allotted 141,600 shares under the employee preferential offering, which came in undersubscribed by 6,600 shares. The shares not taken were reallocated to the Hong Kong public offering.

The company will make its trading debut on the Hong Kong bourse when the opening bell sounds at 9 am on Monday, June 30.

data.title: Nikkei Futures Open Up 400 Points at 40640 on SGX
GenusPlus Secures AU$105 Million Battery Energy Storage Project in South Australia
data.description:
GenusPlus Group received a AU$105 million contract from Alinta Energy to design, supply, build, and commission the 250-megawatt Reeves Plains battery energy storage system in South Australia, according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

Energy storage system with a 275 kilovolt switchyard connecting to ElectraNet's network will be part of Alinta's Reeves Plains energy hub, powering about 300,000 homes, the filing said.

The company's scope covers engineering, equipment supply, civil works, installation, and commissioning, engaging around 100 personnel, the filing added.
